demo.title: seal.sh
demo.code:
# Notarize an action or document with a keyless, tamper-evident seal.
# Content is hashed locally in the browser at /seal — nothing sensitive leaves your machine.
bankr prompt "Use ironbridge-seal to notarize this agreement and give me the shareable /proof URL"

# Read back / confirm a seal by id (free):
bankr prompt "Use ironbridge-seal to look up seal <seal_id> and show its proof page"

# Anyone can re-walk it later for free with ironbridge-verify.

setup: Keyless: seal content at https://ironbridge.foundation/seal — the browser hashes locally, so raw content need not leave your machine.
A seal lands on IronBridge's public chain and gets a human page at /proof/<seal_id>.
Read a seal free: GET /api/seal/<seal_id>. Verify/re-walk free with the ironbridge-verify skill.
Programmatic sealing (verified): GET /api/seal/challenge -> solve PoW (SHA-256(challenge+nonce), >=18 leading zero bits) -> POST /api/seal {content_sha256, type_tag, challenge, nonce}.
